trivia_bullets.text: Modern Horizons 3 (released June 14, 2024) introduced Shield counters and Proliferate as supporting tools for its Modified and Energy themes. Proud Pack-Rhino sits at the white three-drop slot and offers modal flexibility: the Shield counter mode protects a key permanent (modified threshold for Guardian of the Forgotten triggers, energy sources, or a creature with equipment), while the Proliferate mode scales any counter-based engine already running. It is a glue card in the MH3 white draft archetype rather than a top-end payoff. [Scryfall; MTGAZone MH3 White Limited Set Review]
sources: MTGAZone: Modern Horizons 3 MH3 White Limited Set Review

trivia_bullets.text: MTGAZone's MH3 white limited review rated Proud Pack-Rhino 2.5/5, comparing it to Centaur Courser (a 3/3 for {2}{G} with no relevant text) and noting it contributes more incremental value than its surface stats suggest. Its EDHREC rank of 11,582 shows moderate Commander adoption, primarily in +1/+1 counter-proliferate decks where the choose-one entry trigger provides consistent value. The Rhino creature type has modest tribal support (Rhonas, Bronzehide Lion) but no dedicated Rhino commander as of 2024. [MTGAZone MH3 White Limited Set Review; Scryfall EDHREC rank]
sources: MTGAZone: Modern Horizons 3 MH3 White Limited Set Review

trivia_bullets.text: Proud Pack-Rhino is a first printing with no reprint history as of 2025. Art credit: Ron Spears, who also illustrated Guardian of the Forgotten (MH3 no. 28) in this same set. The card carries no flavor text — a common omission for modal ETB commons and uncommons in MH3 where oracle text volume fills the card frame. [Scryfall: artist Ron Spears; no flavor_text field returned]
